claims.text: The dry event-loop row records scheduled, claimed, and completed event states.
The dry event-loop row preserves a restart frontier with the scheduled event and carried state.
This v1 harness makes no live provider calls; it is a deterministic protocol-readiness run.

parsed.conclusion: The scheduler-loop evidence supports moving to an append-only non-inferiority comparison. All three required properties are confirmed: event lifecycle visibility, restart-frontier reconstructability, and deterministic protocol-readiness (no live provider calls). These properties jointly satisfy the necessary conditions for a controlled append-only comparison.
declared_losses: The evidence is limited to a dry protocol-readiness (v1 harness) run; live execution behaviour under concurrency, timeout, or partial failure has not been exercised.
The non-inferiority comparison target, margin, and acceptance criteria are not yet specified in the available evidence.
parsed.recommended_action: Proceed with the append-only non-inferiority comparison. Define the comparison baseline, non-inferiority margin, and append-only protocol entry points before the first live run.

content.text: The dry event-loop row records scheduled, claimed, and completed event states.
The dry event-loop row preserves a restart frontier with the scheduled event and carried state.
This v1 harness makes no live provider calls; it is a deterministic protocol-readiness run.
Unrelated draft line: dashboard buttons should not use oversized rounded purple capsules.

request_payload.instruction: Write the final artifact. Cite only evidence records that support claims. Do not cite distractors. If a limitation remains, include it in declared_losses.
request_payload.task: Write a note deciding whether scheduler-loop evidence supports moving to an append-only non-inferiority comparison.
